items.title: Johor polls will not affect federal government stability, Tok Mat says
items.summary: MUAR: The results of the 16th Johor state election will not affect the stability of the federal government despite the country's changing political landscape and dynamics, said Barisan Nasional deputy chairman Datuk Seri Mohamad Hasan. Mohamad said administrative mandates at the federal and state levels have different moulds, and the current political models in several other states prove that this uniqueness can be practised without causing any issues. Commenting on socio-economic aspects, Mohamad said political stability under BN was critical to ensure Johor continued to drive foreign investment inflows, which are currently ahead of other states.

items.title: Johor voters want stability, cost-of-living focus in state election
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: Johor voters want political stability to remain a priority for the government chosen in the 16th Johor election, alongside a strong focus on the cost of living and job opportunities. Businessman Brian Tan Chong Beng, 67, said a stable government would ensure that existing policies and development projects continue without disruption. Tan, who is the government affairs bureau chief of the Johor Chinese Associations Federation, said Onn Hafiz's leadership qualities justified giving him the opportunity to continue administering the state.

items.title: Perak-born man loses citizenship appeal despite having Malaysian father and passport
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 30 — Perak-born Haritharan Mugunthan, 25, today failed again in court to be declared a Malaysian citizen. This is despite him being born to a Malaysian father, and having once been issued a Malaysian passport when he was just more than one year old. The Court of Appeal unanimously decided today that Haritharan had failed to fulfill the Federal Constitution's requirements to be a Malaysian automatically.

items.title: MARKET PULSE PM JUNE 30, 2026 [WATCH]
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Bursa Malaysia ended lower on Tuesday, extending the previous day's trend, as investors stayed cautious despite a broad-based rally across regional markets. The benchmark index ended the session lower, closing at 1,664 points. In the cryptocurrency market, Bitcoin traded lower at RM241,600, while Ethereum also declined to RM6,457.

items.title: Police hunt for Monaco bomb suspect after Ukrainian-born businessman wounded
items.summary: MONACO: Police on Tuesday were searching for the suspect behind a parcel bomb that wounded a businessman of Ukrainian origin and two others in Monaco, after the unprecedented attack rocked the super-safe principality. Monaco public prosecutor Stephane Thibault said as of Tuesday the man had been stabilised, but the woman's condition remained "life-threatening." Monaco's Minister of State Christophe Mirmand said he was not aware of any specific threats against Yermolaiev.

items.title: World Cup betting: Trio now detained under Immigration Act after remand extension denied
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN: Three foreign men remain in custody under the Immigration Act after the magistrate's court here today rejected police's application to extend their remand. Penang police chief Datuk Dennis Lim said the suspects were initially remanded for four days from June 27 to 30 to facilitate investigations for World Cup football betting activities. He said all the detainees were brought before the George Town magistrate's court for an application to extend their remand by another three days.

items.title: Migration helps a stagnating world to prosper
items.summary: LETTERS: Migration, in its physical, professional, and intellectual forms, is the engine of human life. It is the ultimate mosaic of migration. Take palm oil.

items.title: Tropicana, VitaHealth revive Offline 16 mental wellness initiative for third year
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Tropicana Corp Bhd and Australian-owned supplement brand VitaHealth Malaysia are bringing back their mental wellness initiative, Offline 16, for a third consecutive year as part of efforts to promote healthier digital habits and strengthen community engagement. The programme forms part of both companies' environmental, social and governance (ESG) commitments, with the initiative aimed at encouraging individuals to reduce screen time and reconnect through offline activities and social interaction. Launched as a digital detox campaign, Offline 16 has expanded into a regional mental wellness movement, recording nine editions across Malaysia and Singapore, attracting 1,645 participants and accumulating 46 offline hours.

items.title: Unsubsidised fuel prices cut by 10 sen for July 1-8 period
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: The Finance Ministry announced a 10 sen per litre reduction in unsubsidised RON97, RON95 and diesel prices for the July 1-8 pricing period. RON97 will retail at RM4.00 per litre, while unsubsidised RON95 will be priced at RM3.37 per litre, with unsubsidised diesel reduced to RM3.97 per litre. Subsidised fuel prices remain unchanged, with RON95 under the Budi95 programme maintained at RM1.99 per litre, Budi Diesel at RM2.10 per litre, the Subsidised Petrol Control System at RM2.05 per litre, and the Subsidised Diesel Control System at RM2.15 per litre.

items.title: Strong start for Shah Alam LRT3 as Rapid KL reports 60,000 trips on first full day of service
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 30 — The newly launched Shah Alam Light Rail Transit Line 3 (LRT3) has hit the ground running, recording more than 60,000 passenger trips on its first full day of service this Monday. The figure, according to operator Rapid KL, covers the entire network ecosystem, including train services, feeder buses, and the Rapid KL On-Demand (ROD) service, providing commuters with a seamless, end-to-end journey. Of the total ridership, 58,865 trips were made via train, while 3,319 were recorded across the first-and-last mile network, comprising 1,883 feeder bus trips and 1,436 ROD journeys.

items.title: Bursa Malaysia ends on a softer note
items.summary: Turnover increased to 2.91 billion units valued at RM2.77 billion against 2.71 billion units valued at RM2.13 billion yesterday. Among the top losers, Batu Kawan dropped 34 sen to RM21.10, Allianz trimmed 22 sen to RM21.08, Dynafront tumbled 20 sen to 80 sen, AmBank slid 17 sen to RM6.37, and LPI slipped 16 sen to RM14.98. Warrants turnover expanded to 883.88 million units worth RM111.20 million versus 851.04 million units worth RM110.69 million yesterday.

items.title: Johor polls: 200 Johor PKR members, including vice-chairman, quit for MIC
items.summary: ISKANDAR PUTERI: Two hundred PKR members from its Iskandar Puteri division, including Johor PKR vice chairman M. Murugan, resigned to join MIC on Sunday. State MIC Youth chief A. Mohan said the former PKR members' resignation and subsequent move signalled stronger grassroots support towards Barisan Nasional. "Since the beginning of this year, there have been more than 30,000 new applications to join Johor MIC, including these new members, which brought total nationwide new membership to more than 350,000," he said.

items.title: India's Cockroach party seeking education minister's ouster awaits cabinet reshuffle
items.summary: NEW DELHI: Leaders of India's youth Cockroach Janta Party neared two weeks of sit-in protests on Tuesday, backed by a well-known activist who has started a hunger strike in support of their demand for the resignation of the education minister. The protests in the capital Delhi come as sources say the government is weighing significant cabinet changes, with Indian media reporting Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan could be moved from his portfolio. The CJP, which gained 22 million followers on Instagram within a few days of being set up last month, is demanding his resignation for the leak of question papers for a national medical college entrance examination.

items.title: Ministry, TikTok Shop roll out RM180mil cost relief drive
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Domestic Trade and Cost of Living Ministry and TikTok Shop Malaysia have launched a RM180 million cost-of-living relief initiative. It features lower seller commission fees, shipping rebates and discounts on essential goods. Domestic Trade and Cost of Living Minister Datuk Armizan Mohd Ali said the collaboration shows how digital commerce platforms can support national efforts to promote local products, ease living costs and empower entrepreneurs.

items.title: Mara awaits full police report before acting on Johor MRSM bullying allegations
items.summary: Mara chairman Datuk Dr Asyraf Wajdi Dusuki said the school's disciplinary committee had yet to make a final decision, pending the report from the authorities. Asyraf said Mara would not compromise on discipline, morality and character-building across its education system. Asyraf said Mara would begin placing more former military personnel as full-time wardens at MRSMs from tomorrow to strengthen discipline and student supervision.

items.title: Myanmar denies Asean request for Suu Kyi meeting
items.summary: NAYPYIDAW: Myanmar's authorities have denied a request for Asean's special envoy to meet its deposed and detained democratic leader Aung San Suu Kyi, an administration spokeswoman said Tuesday. The Asean bloc has frozen Myanmar out since the coup but chair nation the Philippines welcomed Suu Kyi's transfer, requesting "brief access" for its Myanmar special envoy. "Aung San Suu Kyi has been prosecuted under the law and is serving sentences," Myanmar presidential office spokeswoman Khaing Khaing Soe told reporters in Naypyidaw.

items.title: MoF cuts unsubsidised fuel prices by 10 sen ahead of BUDI Diesel rollout
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, June 30 — Retail prices for unsubsidised petrol and diesel will drop by 10 sen per litre for the week of July 1 to July 8, the Ministry of Finance announced today. Unsubsidised RON95 will fall to RM3.37 per litre from RM3.47, while unsubsidised diesel drops to RM3.97 per litre from RM4.07. RON95 under the BUDI95 programme stays at RM1.99 per litre, while diesel under BUDI Diesel remains at RM2.10 per litre.

items.title: Court dismisses appeal against Penang reclamation project on procedural grounds
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN: The Court of Appeal today dismissed an appeal by seven fishermen and two environmental groups against the Penang South Reclamation (PSR) project, ruling it was filed too late. The court, however, also held that the director of the Penang Planning Department (PlanMalaysia) and/or the Penang Planning Committee had no power to grant the planning permission for the reclamation project. "The Court of Appeal decided today that the state director of (PlanMalaysia) and/or the Penang Planning Committee had no power to grant the planning permission for the reclamation.

items.title: 60,000 passenger trips on first day of LRT3
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The newly opened Shah Alam LRT3 line recorded 60,000 passenger trips on its first day of operations yesterday. Rapid Rail Sdn Bhd said that this includes overall usage of the Shah Alam Line network, including train services, feeder buses and Rapid KL On-Demand (ROD). Rapid Rail said the response reflected strong demand for the new mobility option.

items.title: Israel-Lebanon deal likely to prolong stalemate, not end war
items.summary: A security deal between Israel and Lebanon risks entrenching a stalemate rather than resolving Israel's underlying conflict with Hizbollah by tying Israel's pullout from southern Lebanon to the Iran-aligned group's disarmament, a condition regional analysts and politicians say is unattainable. With Hizbollah unlikely to disarm, analysts say Israel has political cover to keep an open-ended military presence in southern Lebanon, which it invaded after Hizbollah fired at Israel on March 2 in solidarity with Teheran over the war in Iran. Gerges said Israel had already consolidated a buffer zone in southern Lebanon about 8km to 10km deep while tying any future withdrawal to Hizbollah's disarmament.

items.title: Chin Hin Group announces leadership transition to drive next growth phase
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Chin Hin Group Bhd has announced a strategic leadership transition across its listed companies to reinforce succession continuity and support the next phase of institutional growth. "Concurrently, he will serve as chairman emeritus for Chin Hin Group Property Bhd, Signature International Bhd, Fiamma Holdings Bhd, and Ajiya Bhd. Meanwhile, Datuk Wira Chiau Haw Choon will be appointed as executive chairman of Chin Hin Group, Chin Hin Group Property, Ajiya, Signature International, and Fiamma Holdings.

items.title: Madani book vouchers: 2.2 million students get RM100 each tomorrow
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 30 — More than 2.2 million students under the Ministry of Education (MOE) will benefit from the MADANI Book Voucher Programme 2026, backed by an allocation of RM221.6 million, with redemption of RM100 e-vouchers beginning tomorrow until Oct 31, the Dewan Rakyat was told today. Deputy Education Minister Wong Kah Woh said 2,217,579 recipients comprising students from Remove Class to Form Six, vocational colleges, matriculation colleges and the Malaysian Institute of Teacher Education (IPGM) would benefit from the initiative. Each recipient is required to purchase at least one book from the MADANI Special Title Focus category,” he said during question time.

items.title: Housewife jailed 17 years for causing death of 7-month-old adopted daughter
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A housewife broke down in tears after the Sessions Court here today sentenced her to 17 years' imprisonment for causing the death of her adopted 7-month-old daughter through repeated abuse. Judge Siti Shakirah Mohtarudin handed down the sentence on Siti Subrina Imran, 39, after she pleaded guilty to the charge. Siti Subrina was charged with causing the death of the infant at a condominium unit in Jalan Sri Permaisuri, Cheras, between 10pm on July 29, 2025, and 7am on July 30, 2025.

items.title: Higher electricity prices push Malaysia's headline inflation to 2.0pct in May: Bank Negara
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Higher electricity prices, resulting from a surcharge due to increased generation costs, contributed to Malaysia's headline inflation edging up to 2.0 per cent in May, said Bank Negara Malaysia. In its Monthly Highlights for May 2026 released today, the central bank said headline inflation rose to 2.0 per cent in May from 1.9 per cent in April, while core inflation remained stable at 2.0 per cent. Bank Negara said outstanding corporate bonds grew by 8.0 per cent from 6.2 per cent in April, while outstanding business loans increased by 7.0 per cent from 6.2 per cent previously.

items.title: Johor BN denies involvement in vandalism of Pakatan campaign materials, calls for probe
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, June 30 — Johor Barisan Nasional (BN) has denied any involvement in the incident that involved damaging Pakatan Harapan (PH) flags and campaign materials ahead of the state election. Johor BN information chief Md Israk Abdullah said the state coalition has never ordered, encouraged or sanctioned any form of political vandalism. No party should try to take advantage of this incident to tarnish BN’s name without clear evidence.

items.title: Nearly one million people worldwide became millionaires in 2025, UBS report finds
items.summary: ZURICH: Personal wealth in 2025 grew at its fastest pace in years, creating nearly one million new US dollar millionaires worldwide, Swiss bank UBS said in its annual Global Wealth Report published on Tuesday. Here are some details: Total personal wealth globally rose by 10.8 per cent last year, up from 4.6 per cent in 2024 and 4.2 per cent in 2023, as strong financial markets boosted growth, UBS found. While average wealth rose, inequality has deepened since 2020, UBS said.

items.title: Australia reports 5th mainland case of H5N1 bird flu
items.summary: CANBERRA: Australian authorities on Tuesday confirmed the fifth mainland case of the deadly H5N1 strain of avian influenza, but said there is no evidence of local transmission, Xinhua reported. Minister for Agriculture, Fisheries, and Forestry, Julie Collins, and Chief Veterinary Officer, Beth Cookson, told reporters in Canberra that testing has detected the H5N1 strain in a migratory giant petrel that was found at a beach on the south coast of Western Australia (WA). It takes the total number of confirmed cases of the deadly strain in mainland Australia to five, four of which have been detected in WA.

items.title: Syed Saddiq's lawyer calls Federal Court postponement 'unprecedented' as ruling delayed
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: Syed Saddiq Syed Abdul Rahman's lawyer, Datuk Hisyam Teh Poh Teik, described today's Federal Court postponement as "unprecedented," saying it was the first time in his legal career that he had experienced such a situation. He said he respected the court's decision to postpone the ruling, noting that the delay was due to unforeseen circumstances. The Federal Court has fixed July 13 for its decision.

items.title: From stray dogs to Wi-Fi: Pakatan candidate Guna Balakrishnan lists the top headaches for Layang-Layang voters
items.summary: The Layang-Layang state seat witnessed a three-cornered contest involving Guna, Barisan Nasional (BN) candidate Chua Jian Boon and incumbent Abd Mutalip Abd Rahim, representing Perikatan Nasional (PN). Abd Mutalip Abd Rahim (PN-Bersatu) 3. Chua Jian Boon (BN-MCA) (2022: Abd Mutalip Abd Rahim: BN-Umno: Maj: 2,815) — Bernama

items.title: Myanmar jade mine landslide kills five, 15 missing
items.summary: YANGON: Monsoon rains triggered the collapse of slag heap at a disused Myanmar jade mine, killing five people scavenging for gem scraps and leaving around 15 more buried missing, state media said Tuesday. "Around 20 freelance jade scavengers searching the site under floodlights were buried when the heap suddenly gave way" late on Sunday in Kachin's Hpakant township, state media said. "The collapse occurred after days of heavy rain left old mine waste heaps unstable," said newspaper The Global New Light of Myanmar.

items.title: NUJ joins calls to refer Cybercrimes Bill to select committee, cites need to protect journalists' sources
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The National Union of Journalists Malaysia (NUJM) has called for the Cybercrimes Bill 2026 to be referred to a parliamentary special select committee (PSSC), saying further scrutiny is needed to ensure the law does not undermine journalists' ability to protect confidential sources. NUJM representative Teh Athira Mohd Yusof said stakeholders affected by the bill must be consulted to provide insights and clarify concerns before the legislation is passed. NGOs Article 19, the Centre for Independent Journalism (CIJ) and Sinar Project expressed concerns that the cybercrime legislation failed to meet minimum standards set out in the United Nations Cybercrime Convention.

items.title: MOH shuts down own website temporarily after cyber scare, says patient data safe
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, June 30 — The Ministry of Health (MOH) has temporarily suspended access to its official website to allow for enhanced cybersecurity measures to be implemented. “The ministry’s healthcare delivery systems continue to operate as normal and are protected by separate infrastructure with strict cybersecurity controls,” it said. On Saturday, media reports said the MOH official portal experienced access disruptions believed to be linked to a cybersecurity incident.

items.title: Army sets up inquiry board, suspends heavy weapons live-fire training after Hobart Camp incident leaves two soldiers hurt
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 30 — The Malaysian Army has formed a board of inquiry to determine the cause of the incident involving two soldiers who were injured during a live-fire training exercise at Hobart Camp in Gurun, Kedah, yesterday. Army Chief General Tan Sri Azhan Md Othman said all live-fire training involving support weapons has also been temporarily suspended pending the outcome of the investigation, BuletinTV3 reported today. He said the special board will examine three key areas — human factors, technical issues, and compliance with standard operating procedures (SOPs).

items.title: Fuziah wishes ex-PKR members well after mass exit, says she hopes they get senior posts
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: PKR secretary-general Datuk Dr Fuziah Salleh has brushed off the departure of former Johor PKR state leadership council vice-chairman M. Murugan and his supporters, downplaying their decision as being driven by frustration over positions. Commenting on Murugan's announcement that he and around 200 supporters left the party to join the Iskandar Puteri MIC division, Fuziah said she found the reasoning behind their exit strange. Two days ago, Murugan, popularly known as 'Cikgu Rony', reportedly said the decision was made out of frustration with both the state and central PKR leadership, claiming they had been indifferent toward long-standing complaints.

items.title: Man arrested in Kota Kinabalu hotel over Lawas parang attack
items.summary: Lawas police chief DSP Liong Tindan said the suspect was arrested at 2am by a team from the district Criminal Investigation Department and their Sabah contingent counterparts. "Police tracked down the suspect before raiding the hotel room and arresting him," Liong said. Liong said the suspect tested positive for methamphetamine and amphetamine.

items.title: Thai Airways crew member arrested in Australia over heroin haul
items.summary: SYDNEY: A Thai airline employee was charged with importing more than a kilogram (2.2 pounds) of heroin into Australia after customs officials found the drug concealed in tote bags, Australian Federal Police said. The 26-year-old woman, who national flag carrier Thai Airways said was working as a flight attendant when she arrived at Melbourne Airport on June 25, had 12 tote bags screened by border officers, police said. Australian Border Force Commander Clint Sims said criminal syndicates continued to target trusted insiders, including airline crew, to smuggle illicit substances into Australia.

items.title: Over nine million STR recipients now eligible for mySalam health payouts, govt mulls extension
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 30 — A total of 9.15 million Sumbangan Tunai Rahmah (STR) recipients are eligible for benefits under the MySalam B40 National Protection Scheme based on this year’s criteria. Finance Minister II Datuk Seri Amir Hamzah Azizan said that since its launch in 2019 until Dec 31, 2025, some 1.88 million individuals have received payouts totalling RM1.42 billion. With a remaining fund balance of RM490.9 million as of end-2025, the scheme remains well-positioned to continue supporting its target group.

items.title: South Korea, Ukraine hold 'constructive' talks about North Korean POWs
items.summary: SEOUL: South Korea and Ukraine held "constructive" talks Tuesday about the fate of two North Korean soldiers captured while fighting in Russia, Seoul said Tuesday. South Korean Foreign Minister Cho Hyun and Ukrainian counterpart Andriy Sybiga discussed the matter during talks in Seoul. North and South Korea remain technically at war after their 1950-53 conflict ended in an armistice, not a peace treaty.

items.title: Man dies after falling from 14th floor apartment in Kuching
items.summary: Berita Harian reported that in the incident at about 2.10pm, the victim was found lodged at the fourth floor of the building before being confirmed dead by Health Ministry (KKM) personnel at the scene. A spokesperson for the Sarawak Fire and Rescue Department Operations Centre said they received an emergency call regarding the incident at 2.15pm. He said a team from the Batu Lintang Fire and Rescue Station was immediately deployed to the location to carry out rescue operations.

items.title: Selangor Intelligent Parking system hit by outage
items.summary: SHAH ALAM: The Selangor Intelligent Parking (SIP) system has been hit by a temporary outage, affecting several services, particularly parking payment functions. Rantaian Mesra Sdn Bhd (RMSB), a company owned by Menteri Besar Selangor (Incorporation), said the SIP platform had been temporarily suspended to facilitate recovery efforts and safeguard system and user data integrity. In a statement, RMSB said technical inspections were underway to ensure the system was stable and met safety, data integrity and reliability standards before services resumed.

items.title: Wet weather alert: Thunderstorms, heavy rain expected in KL, Putrajaya and 11 states until 7pm, says MetMalaysia
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 30 — The Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) has issued a thunderstorm warning for several states, with heavy rain, lightning and strong winds expected until 7pm today. In a post on Facebook, MetMalaysia said affected areas include Perlis; Kedah (Langkawi, Kubang Pasu, Kota Setar, Pokok Sena, Padang Terap, Yan, Baling, Kulim and Bandar Baharu); Penang (South Seberang Perai); Perak (Kerian); Pahang (Raub, Bentong, Bera and Rompin); Selangor (Klang, Gombak, Petaling, Kuala Langat, Hulu Langat and Sepang); the Federal Territories of Kuala Lumpur and Putrajaya; Negeri Sembilan; Melaka; and Johor (Tangkak, Segamat, Muar and Mersing). In East Malaysia, the warning covers Sarawak’s Samarahan, Betong, Sarikei, Sibu, Mukah, Kapit (Song), Bintulu (Tatau), Miri (Subis) and Limbang, as well as Sabah’s Interior (Sipitang, Tenom and Nabawan), West Coast (Ranau), Tawau (Tawau and Lahad Datu) and Sandakan (Telupid, Kinabatangan, Beluran and Sandakan).

items.title: Cheaper and faster: How the new LRT3 is changing life at UiTM Shah Alam
items.summary: SHAH ALAM, June 30 — The operation of the Light Rail Transit 3 (LRT3) Shah Alam Line has delivered significant benefits to students of Universiti Teknologi MARA (UiTM) Shah Alam by improving daily mobility and helping reduce traffic congestion around the campus area. He was speaking after launching the Semiconductor@UiTM initiative at the Tuanku Abdul Halim Mu’adzam Shah Engineering Complex, UiTM Shah Alam. The Shah Alam Line comprises 20 stations: Bandar Utama, Kayu Ara, BU11 (Bandar Utama 11), Damansara Idaman, Subang, Glenmarie 2, Kerjaya, Stadium Shah Alam, Dato’ Menteri, UiTM Shah Alam, Section 7 Shah Alam, Bandar Baru Klang, Pasar Klang, Jalan Meru, Jambatan Kota, Taman Selatan, Seri Andalas, Klang Jaya, Bandar Bukit Tinggi and Johan Setia.

items.title: Another incident at Hobart Camp, two soldiers injured after weapon explodes
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 30 — Two military personnel, a corporal and a sergeant, sustained knee injuries after a firearm used during training at Hobart Camp in Gurun, Kedah, exploded due to a technical malfunction yesterday. Kedah police chief Datuk Adzli Abu Shah said the injuries were confirmed not to have been caused by bullets, but rather by fragments or foreign objects propelled when the weapon suddenly exploded. “Preliminary investigations found that a technical issue occurred when the weapon experienced stoppages or malfunctioned during training.

items.title: Home again: Malaysian Tak Bai bomb survivors now under treatment in Kota Bharu hospital
items.summary: KOTA BHARU, June 30 — Two Malaysian men who were injured in a bomb explosion in Tak Bai, Narathiwat, southern Thailand yesterday were transferred to Raja Perempuan Zainab II Hospital (HRPZII) in Kota Bharu today for further medical treatment. HRPZII director Dr Izamin Idris said the victims, Abdullah Syarapi Abd Rahman, 45, and Muhammad Yusri Udin, 38, would first undergo assessment at the Emergency Department before receiving subsequent treatment. “The victims arrived at the hospital at 10.50 am today from Narathiwat Ratchanakarin Hospital via the Rantau Panjang Immigration, Customs, Quarantine and Security (ICQS) Complex.

items.title: UM staff fail in final bid to challenge compulsory Covid-19 vaccination circular
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, June 30 — A group of Universiti Malaya (UM) staff failed to obtain Federal Court leave to appeal the dismissal of their challenge against the government’s circular requiring compulsory Covid-19 vaccination. On October 27, 2021, the UM staff as well as several teachers from different government schools filed their judicial review application against six respondents, namely the education minister, education director-general, Public Service Department (PSD) director-general, the health minister, health director-general and Malaysian government. The group was initially granted leave by the High Court in May 2022 to commence a judicial review challenging the circular.

items.title: Woman found in suitcase in Kajang apartment died from slash to the head, say cops
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 30 — The woman who was found stuffed inside a suitcase in an apartment in Kajang, Selangor, died after being slashed in the head, police said today. Kajang police chief Naazron Abdul Yusof said the post-mortem was completed yesterday evening and confirmed that the victim’s body was found intact, according to Free Malaysia Today . Police later found the victim stuffed inside a suitcase in the living room.

items.title: From orchard to ice-cream: Fama buys up the glut to stop Terengganu’s durians from rotting
items.summary: KUALA TERENGGANU, June 30 — The Terengganu Federal Agricultural Marketing Authority (Fama) has begun processing durians into pulp daily to address the oversupply of the fruit in the state’s market. Its director, Muhamad Fadlee Hassan, said the initiative, carried out at Fama’s Fresh Fruit Processing Centre in Besut since June 22, forms part of the 2026 Seasonal Fruits Marketing and Intervention Implementation Plan to ensure growers’ produce is marketed more systematically while addressing excess supply. Muhamad Fadlee said the processing initiative was introduced after farm-gate durian prices fell below RM3.50 per kg.

items.title: Court of Appeal grants Malaysian citizenship to abandoned Klang teen, rejects Pahang woman’s appeal
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 30 — The Court of Appeal in a 2-1 decision today ruled that a 17-year-old stateless girl, who was abandoned by her unknown biological mother a few days after being born in a clinic in Klang, Selangor, is a Malaysian and should be given a MyKad or Malaysian identity card. But the Court of Appeal today unanimously decided that a 29-year-old stateless woman who was abandoned as a one-year-old baby at a bus stop in Pahang cannot automatically be a Malaysian, as she was not a “new born” child when she was abandoned. What the Klang girl (M) won in her citizenship case Court of Appeal judges Datuk Faizah Jamaludin and Datuk Hayatul Akmal Abdul Aziz today decided that the girl M, who will turn 18 later this year, is automatically entitled to Malaysian citizenship under the Federal Constitution.

items.title: Larkin for a comeback: Pakatan candidate Suhaizan Kaiat bets on high turnout to flip the seat
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, June 30 — Pakatan Harapan (PH) candidate for the Larkin seat, Suhaizan Kaiat, is optimistic about recapturing the seat from Barisan Nasional (BN) in the 16th Johor state election. In GE14, the Larkin seat was won by Datuk Mohd Izhar Ahmad, who contested under the PH (Bersatu) banner, before BN’s Mohd Hairi Mad Shah reclaimed the seat in the 2022 Johor state election. Mohd Hairi Mad Shah (BN-Umno) (2022: Mohd Hairi Mad Shah: BN-Umno: Majority: 6,178) — Bernama

items.title: MOF: Health Ministry spending adjustment capped at RM500m, not RM3.1b as claimed online
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 30 — The Ministry of Finance (MOF) has considered an adjustment to the Health Ministry’s operating expenditure of up to RM500 million, far below the RM3.1 billion figure circulating on social media, said Finance Minister II Datuk Seri Amir Hamzah Azizan. He said the amount does not involve core spending, including emoluments, medical supplies, overtime payments and on-call allowances. Amir Hamzah said the expenditure adjustment only covers non-critical operating costs, while all core spending, including essential services, security, healthcare and education, would continue as approved under Budget 2026.

items.title: From massage to magistrate: Kota Kinabalu therapist in hot water over 'inappropriate' bodywork
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, June 30 — A 31-year-old massage therapist was charged in the Magistrate’s Court here to molesting a male client during a body massage session. The charge under Section 354 of the Penal Code carries a jail term of up to 10 years, or a fine, or whipping, or any two of the punishments, on conviction. The court fixed Aug 5 for pre-trial case management and released the accused on bail of RM3,000 with two sureties.

items.title: Dewan Rakyat passes Sexual Offences Against Children amendment Bill 2026, closing overseas loopholes
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 30 — The Dewan Rakyat today passed the Sexual Offences Against Children (Amendment) Bill 2026, extending the Act’s jurisdiction to cover offences committed outside Malaysia. Deputy Minister in the Prime Minister’s Department (Law and Institutional Reform) M. Kulasegaran said the amendment to expand extraterritorial application will ensure that offenders with ties to Malaysia cannot evade justice simply because their crimes were committed overseas. This amendment aims to prevent overlapping proceedings and ensure that offenders do not slip through the legal net,” he said.

items.title: From RM85 to RM30: Government picks up the health check tab for truckers to keep roads safe
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 30 — Effective July 1, commercial vehicle drivers in Malaysia will be eligible for a subsidised medical examination when they renew their vocational licence annually. Currently, drivers are required to pay RM85 for the medical evaluation. Loke said the initiative, implemented under Budget 2026, costs RM12.4 million, comprising RM7 million from Socso, while the Transport Ministry will bear the remaining RM5.4 million.

items.title: From Henry Gurney to jail: Court swaps teen driver’s reform school for prison term after fatal joyride in Kuching
items.summary: KUCHING, June 30 — The High Court has substituted a teenage boy’s six-year detention at the Henry Gurney School for causing a fatal multi-vehicle crash on Jalan Teo Kui Seng here in January last year with six years’ imprisonment. He was charged under Section 41(1) of the Road Transport Act 1987 with reckless driving causing the deaths of five people: George Herson Andrew, 15, Japin Kemin, 19, Nasrulhakim Mohamad Sedek, 41, Caryn Anne Risly, 35, and Wan Khairul Nizam Wan Abdul Kadir, 41. The court further held that a three-year detention at the reform school would be manifestly inadequate for an offence under Section 41(1) of the Road Transport Act 1987.

items.title: Ghost in the machine: Kota Kinabalu City Hall says parking on the house after app breaks down
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, June 30 — Motorists using public parking spaces in the city will not be penalised for unpaid parking fees following a temporary disruption affecting the Sabah Smart Parking (SSP) application. In a notice issued on Tuesday, DBKK Holdings Sdn Bhd said the SSP application is currently experiencing operational issues, affecting several key services relied upon by users for daily parking transactions. The temporary suspension effectively provides a parking fee holiday for motorists using city parking bays managed through the SSP system until the application resumes normal operations.

items.title: ‘Think wisely, Johoreans’: A vote for BN could mean a vote for PAS, says PKR sec-gen
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, June 29 — Parti Keadilan Rakyat (PKR) secretary-general Datuk Fuziah Salleh today said Johoreans should keep in mind that voting for Umno-Barisan Nasional (BN) in the upcoming state election could also translate into support for Parti Islam Se-Malaysia (PAS). “Johor voters need to think wisely. Fuziah said PAS’ move reflected divisions within PN, which she said would benefit Pakatan Harapan (PH).

items.title: Under eight minutes just the start: Transport minister promises shorter wait time for LRT3 trains if Shah Alam crowds surge
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 30 — Prasarana will gradually increase the frequency of trains operating along the LRT3 Line once the demand picks up and after the new batch of trains arrive, Transport Minister Anthony Loke said today. He said the frequency of trains along the Shah Alam Line will be revised based on the trend of usage after it operates for one whole month. “Currently, the frequency stands about 7.5 minutes at peak hours.

items.title: No new projects, no new promises: PM Anwar draws the line on election handouts
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 30 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has reiterated that the announcement of new projects or policies during election campaigns is prohibited under election laws. He said the restriction is in line with Section 24B of the Election Offences Act 1954. “Government machinery must not be used to announce new projects during the campaign period, whether by city councils, state governments or the Federal Government,” he said during the Minister’s Question Time in the Dewan Rakyat today.

items.title: Johor polls: Bersama skips manifesto, unveils five-point voter pledge
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 30 — Parti Bersama Malaysia today unveiled a five-point service pledge for the Johor state election, saying it would focus on commitments it could realistically deliver rather than issuing a traditional election manifesto. According to The Star , party president Datuk Seri Rafizi Ramli said the approach reflected Bersama’s position as a new party contesting only 15 state seats and its aim of becoming a credible opposition rather than forming the state government. “This is a state election, and the powers of the Federal and state governments are clearly defined under the Federal Constitution.

items.title: Hope you get the posts you wanted: PKR sec-gen Fuziah tells ex-members who joined MIC
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, June 30 — The action by a group of PKR leaders and members to leave the party and join MIC recently has been described as “rather strange” by PKR secretary-general Datuk Dr Fuziah Salleh. She said the party’s internal findings indicated that the move was driven by dissatisfaction over not being appointed to positions within the party. On June 28, former Johor PKR State Leadership Council vice-chairman M. Murugan announced that he and about 200 supporters had left the party to join the MIC Iskandar Puteri division.
